projects.title: CITES Social Media — Species Spotlight & Nature's Pharmacy (Unofficial Concept Samples)
projects.role: Visual Designer · Social Media Concept Creator
projects.background: **Concept social media posts** I designed to support my application to the **CITES Secretariat** internship. The set spans two parallel series: a LinkedIn carousel on CITES-listed medicinal plants ("Nature's Pharmacy") and an Instagram trilingual series on the snowdrop ("Species Spotlight"). Both were published on my personal account, with a clear disclaimer that the posts are concept samples and are not official CITES content or endorsed by the Secretariat.
projects.contribution: I served as the **visual designer and social media concept creator** on this project, handling every visual in the set — from copy and layout to typography and image treatment, working in **Adobe Photoshop**. I built two parallel series — a three-slide LinkedIn carousel on CITES-listed medicinal plants (American ginseng, Hoodia, and African cherry), and a three-language Instagram series on the snowdrop (English, French, Spanish) — translating technical species information into accessible, social-first posts.

galleries.title: Instagram · Species Spotlight — The Snowdrop (EN / FR / ES)

projects.outcomes:
Published on my personal social accounts as **concept samples** accompanying my application, with a clear disclaimer that they are not official CITES content. The internship outcome is pending.

Software and Design Process
All images were designed in **Adobe Photoshop**. My process and main considerations were:
- I studied **CITES's own social media posts** to understand their tone and visual style, and designed the images to be consistent with it — with clear species labelling (common name plus the scientific name in italics), the CITES Appendix level, and a credit line for each photo.
- I adapted each sample to its platform: the **LinkedIn** piece is a multi-image carousel with a longer caption, and the **Instagram** piece focuses on a single species with the on-image text in the three official CITES languages — **English**, **French** and **Spanish**.
- All photos are from **Wikimedia Commons** under open licenses (public domain, CC BY, CC BY-SA). I credited each one and, for share-alike images, noted the adaptation and matched the licence.
- I also wrote **alt text** for the images, as an accessibility step.
- Every claim printed on the images is backed by a **source**.

projects.title: CASHEW: The Golden Opportunity West Africa Is Giving Away
projects.background: **An AI-generated documentary** that follows the journey of the cashew nut — from the fertile lands of Sierra Leone to the global processing markets of India and Vietnam — to expose a value-addition gap at the heart of West Africa's economy. Africa grows more than half of the world's raw cashews yet processes less than 10% of its own harvest, so the industrial value, and the millions of jobs that come with it, flow overseas. The film makes the case that shifting from raw exports to local processing can create jobs, strengthen communities, and turn agriculture into a pathway to industrialisation.
projects.contribution: I served as the **video editor and AI content creator** on this project, taking a written script and turning it into a finished cinematic documentary. I built every shot through an image-to-video pipeline — generating cinematic frames of West African landscapes, factories and farming communities with ByteDance's **Seedream** (via Jimeng), then animating them through **Pavo** — before integrating AI voiceover and handling all editing, pacing, motion graphics and sound design in **CapCut**. The project was created for the **Twenty-First Century African Youth Movement** through the **United Nations Online Volunteering Service**.
projects.outcomes: Published on the **Voices of the EMERGING Africa** YouTube channel, the documentary now anchors the movement's advocacy — reframing agriculture as a gateway to industrialisation for policymakers, investors and young entrepreneurs, and showing that a grassroots, AI-driven production can hold its own against global media standards.

projects.title: The Road Leads to a Summer Dream Unfinished
projects.background: The Road Leads to a Summer Dream Unfinished is **a documentary** chronicling a 2,000-kilometre summer expedition organised by the Cycling Association of Peking University, tracing a cross-regional ride from Guangxi to Yunnan. Beyond preserving the journey, the film set out to translate the spirit of the expedition — endurance, companionship, and the pull of the open road — into visual storytelling that could travel beyond the team itself. It also doubled as a recruitment and outreach piece, introducing the association to prospective members and a wider audience.
projects.contribution: I led the project from end to end — shaping the narrative as **director**, capturing footage both on the ground and from the air as **cinematographer** and **aerial operator**, and handling the entire post-production — including editing, sound, and colour — in **Adobe Premiere Pro** and **CapCut**. I also appear on camera. From first concept to final cut, the film carries a single, consistent creative vision.
projects.outcomes: Released across multiple platforms, including **Bilibili**, **TikTok** and **YouTube**, the film has gathered over 10,000 views in total. Within the association and across campus it circulated as a shared record of the expedition, and it continues to support the club's recruitment and outreach — reinforcing its visibility and sense of community.

projects.title: National Collegiate Cycling Assembly
projects.background: **The key visual** for a national assembly of collegiate cycling associations across China — a large-scale gathering uniting university cycling clubs nationwide. As the event's principal poster, it needed to establish a strong, recognisable identity capable of anchoring the entire event and carrying its spirit across both online and on-site touchpoints.
projects.contribution: I independently developed the entire design as **lead designer**, working in **Adobe Photoshop** and **Adobe Illustrator** — from concept and visual direction through composition to final artwork. The poster went on to become the visual foundation for a broader system of collateral, including the guidebook, invitations, and other secondary materials, all derived from this central key visual.
projects.outcomes: As the event's primary visual, the poster reached a wide audience through both online circulation and on-site display, served as **the main stage backdrop**, and provided dedicated exposure for sponsors. Its visual language extended across the event's full suite of communication materials, giving the assembly a consistent, professional identity.

projects.title: Silk Road
projects.background: **A custom garment** commissioned for the expedition's sponsors — at once a contractual deliverable, a token of appreciation, and the official identity wear of the expedition team. It was made to be worn at formal occasions after the team crossed into Kazakhstan, giving sponsors visible, cross-border exposure.
projects.contribution: I handled the project independently as **designer** and **client liaison**, from concept and original artwork through to layout, while liaising directly with the sponsors to confirm the design met their needs. Building on the Silk Road theme, I created an original visual in **Adobe Illustrator**, **Adobe Photoshop**, **Midjourney** and **Jimeng AI** that fuses classic motifs — a camel caravan and a traditional seal — with desert textures and silk patterns artistically interwoven, and finished with bilingual Chinese–Russian typography suited to its cross-border setting.
projects.outcomes: Adopted by the sponsors and worn by the team as **official identity apparel**, the design delivered sponsor exposure at formal occasions during the cross-border expedition into Kazakhstan — serving both its branding purpose and its role as a unifying team symbol.

projects.title: Milan Winter Olympics
projects.background: An AI-generated short film created for a global AIGC competition tied to the Milan–Cortina Winter Olympics.
projects.contribution: I conceived and produced the film end to end — from concept and prompt design through to generation and editing — exploring figure skating through an AI-driven visual language.

projects.title: Promotional Flag
projects.background: **A promotional flag** created for the cycling association's expedition — not a commercial piece, but the team's visual emblem. It travelled with the team as a guiding banner at the head of the formation during the ride, and stood front and centre as the rallying symbol held up in group photographs at events along the way.
projects.contribution: I designed the flag in full as **designer**, working in **Adobe Photoshop**, from concept and visual direction through to final artwork. The brief was to distil the team's identity into a single, instantly recognisable mark that would read clearly from a distance, hold up while in motion at the head of the column, and anchor every group shot it appeared in.
projects.outcomes: Carried throughout the expedition as the team's identity, the flag gained wider exposure when it appeared in coverage of the journey — featured both in **People's Daily** and on official reporting channels, extending the team's visibility well beyond the route itself.

projects.title: If the River Wind Forgets
projects.background: A 600-kilometre expedition film documenting a winter cycling journey from Hunan to Guangxi, China.
projects.contribution: I shot the footage both on the ground and from the air, then handled the edit to shape it into a self-contained record of the ride.

projects.title: 2025 China–Kazakhstan Expedition
projects.background: Official team apparel for the 2025 China–Kazakhstan Expedition.
projects.contribution: I designed the garment independently, turning the expedition's theme into the team's identity wear for the cross-border ride.

projects.title: 2024 Summer Expedition
projects.background: Official team apparel for the 2024 Summer Expedition.
projects.contribution: I designed the piece independently as the team's identity wear, translating the spirit of the summer ride into a shared, recognisable look.

projects.title: Department of Psychology
projects.background: An official hoodie for the Department of Psychology.
projects.contribution: I designed it independently, working a brain motif into the artwork so the piece carries the department's discipline as a visible part of its identity.

projects.title: Album Cover
projects.background: Album cover artwork developed as an independent conceptual piece.
projects.contribution: I created the cover from concept to finish, exploring mood and atmosphere through composition and colour.

projects.title: Shangri-La
projects.background: A postcard built from my own photography of the Shangri-La landscape.
projects.contribution: I shot the original images and refined them in post, composing them into a postcard that heightens the high-altitude atmosphere of the scenery.

projects.title: City God
projects.background: A postcard depicting the City God (Chenghuang) of Chinese folk tradition.
projects.contribution: I hand-drew the piece from scratch, reinterpreting a classical guardian figure in an original illustrated style.

projects.title: Teacher
projects.background: A postcard built around the classical Chinese imagery of the teacher–student relationship.
projects.contribution: I hand-drew the illustration, drawing on traditional master–disciple imagery to convey the weight and warmth of that bond.

projects.title: Bipolar Disorder
projects.background: A poster I designed after visiting a psychiatric hospital, conveying the struggle of living with bipolar disorder.
projects.contribution: I designed it independently, using visual metaphor and contrast to externalise the swing between highs and lows and to draw wider attention to the condition.

projects.title: Money or Poison
projects.background: A postcard inspired by The Wolf of Wall Street, reflecting on wealth and value.
projects.contribution: I designed the piece independently, using imagery to question where wealth ends and excess begins.

projects.title: Project Report
projects.background: A cover visual for an academic project report on parent–child conflict.
projects.contribution: I designed the cover to translate the report's theme of family tension into a clear, presentable image.

projects.title: Solar System
projects.background: A vector graphic piece built around the solar system.
projects.contribution: I illustrated it in Adobe Illustrator, using clean geometric forms to render the planets as a cohesive visual set.

projects.title: Wedding Invitation Card
projects.background: A bespoke wedding invitation commissioned by a private client.
projects.contribution: I designed it to the couple's brief — an early step toward turning personal creative practice into paid work.

projects.title: Rural Education Revolution in Africa
projects.background: A short AI-generated documentary made for the Twenty-First Century African Youth Movement (AYM) and its Voices of the EMERGING Africa initiative. Across rural Africa, countless children dream of becoming doctors and engineers yet lack qualified science teachers and learning resources. Told through the eyes of a child, the film shares a perspective on hope, learning, and the future — and shows how AYM's hybrid model, pairing physical community centres with mobile technology, is working to close that gap.
projects.contribution: I produced the film end to end and entirely on my own — from concept and scripting through AI-generated visuals to the final cut. I built each shot with an image-to-video pipeline — generating the key frames with ByteDance's Seedream (via Jimeng) and bringing them to life through Pavo — then paired the footage with AI narration and assembled, paced and scored the whole piece in CapCut, aiming for a grassroots, youth-led story that still holds a cinematic standard.
projects.outcomes: Released on YouTube as part of AYM's youth-led advocacy for the Rural Education Revolution, the documentary was made to carry the story to a wider audience of supporters, partners and prospective donors — and to show that AI-assisted, independently produced film can bring a global-quality voice to a grassroots cause.
